Subject: Public Meeting Conducted Outside of City Limits
To V{hom It May Concern:
t have bgen a Rolling Hills Estates Councilwoman (located on the
Palos Verdes Peninsula) since March, 1989.
Shortly after my election to this position, there was a City
council Retreat (posted as a publ ic meeting) held in Palm Springs,
California. The 5 members of the city Counci l (including myself),
the Cify Manager and the Assjstant City Manager spent a week end
in Palm Springs discussing City policy. The meeting was not tape-
recorded; the City Manager and Assistant City Manager took notes
of what was being discussed. Since I was a newly elected official,
I went, but with many reservations about the fact that there was
going to be a public meeting held with City business being
discussed and it would be held approximately 150 miles outside of
our City limits.
While at that Retreat, there were many items discussed. One of the
items discuased was regarding a highly controversial issuo which
had outraged many citizens in Rolling Hills Estates--the sale of
school property to a home developer by the School Board of the
Palos Verdes Peninsu'la. This school property is located in Rolling
Hills Estates and is the Dapplegray School Site, After discussion
of this item took place, I was left with the impression by comments
made by the City Manager that the fu]1 discussions of this highly
controversia'l issue would not be contained in the minutes of the
meeti ng.
Upon returning from th€ Retreat, the minutes of the meeting were
compi'led and dissemjnated to the Council members for corrections
and review, Upon reading the minutes, I realized that the fulI
discussions of this h1gh1y controversial issue were indeed not
fully reported in the minutes of this Retreat.
At the next Council Meeting (which was a public meeting held in
Rolling H'i lls Estates), I challenged the content of the mtnutes and
had them corrected to include the full discussions of this hi9h1y
controversia'l issue. Since there was no tape recording of this
Retreat, I was forced to rely on my own personal notes to have the
minutes corrected to the best of my ability. 
The minutes were
corrected to my satisfaction, but this was not an easy task to
accomplish. I have in my possession a copy of the minutes as Fair

first presented without alI of the information included, and a copy
of the amended minutes which inc'ludes the sensitive Dappl69ray
school site di scussions.
Now, approximately a year later, anothgr Counci l Retreat is being
planned for the first week ehd in March of th'is year. It again
will be held in Palm Springs, approximate'ly 150 miies outs'ide of
our Ci ty l imits.
I have, at pub'l ic city Council Me€tings, expressed my disp'leasure
at having officially convensd pub] ic moetings of the City Counci l
held outside of the city, 
It would be quite difficult for any
residents of our City to attend this public meeting which is going
to be he I d 'l 50 mi 'l e€ away f rom hom€ .
I do not plan on attending this weekend Council Retreat 1n Palm
Springs. If this onco a year Retreat is going to take place, as
it has in the past, goodngSs knows where the next one will be. It
could take p'lace in New Orleans, Louisiana or Anchorage, Alagka for
al'l I know.
My questions to you are:
Is it legal and/or p.oper to conduct a Council Retreat (posted as
a public meeting) 150 mi'les outside of our City?
I€ it appropriate that there bE no tape recording of the meeting?
I would certain'ly appreciate the answers to my questions at your
earliest convenience. Please contact me at my home with your
response and any questions. My address is as reflected above and
my lelephone humber is (213) 373-8200.
Si ncerel y,

sections.question: Is it legal and/or proper to conduct a Council Retreat (posted as a public meeting) 150 miles outside of our City? Is it appropriate that there be no tape recording of the meeting?

sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asks whether it is legally proper for a city council to hold posted public meetings (retreats) 150 miles outside city limits and whether the absence of tape recording at such meetings is appropriate.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: This is an incoming request/complaint letter, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or advice section is present. The document is dated February 17, 1990 and appears to be the original inquiry sent to the FPPC. The requestor explicitly poses two questions at the end of the letter.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Is it legal and/or proper to conduct a Council Retreat (posted as a public meeting) 150 miles outside of our City? Is it appropriate that there be no tape recording of the meeting?

embedding.summary: A Rolling Hills Estates Councilwoman writes to the FPPC expressing concerns about city council retreats held in Palm Springs, approximately 150 miles outside city limits, and questions the legality and propriety of such distant public meetings and the lack of tape recording.
